oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Crossword Puzzle Solution
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Jordan, Rick
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 2 (April 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.The illegal trafficking of parrots in the region has increased since the wildlife officials have placed a prohibition on the management of parrots and macaws by non-governmental organizations. In the Lacandon Jungle alone, it is estimated that between 90 and 100 chicks are poached annually. The lack of opportunities in the region and the poor execution of public policies have led an opportunistic sector of the population to engage in this illegal activity. In addition, the lack of government authorities responsible for environmental protection has made this region a highly vulnerable site for the scarlet macaw and parrots of the Lacandon Jungle.Sebastian Arriago and Rick Jordan inspect a wild Scarlet baby before returning it to the nest.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 2 (2023): Watchbird April 2023; 21
